question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=gte:0` filter to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()` to retrieve all staking reward transactions from the genesis of the network, effectively enumerating the complete staking transaction history for any account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id` that equals the target account's own ID to `spenderIdInFilters`, causing the query to return NFT transaction records where the account has approved itself, potentially revealing self-approval transactions that indicate unusual account behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id=gte:X` without a `serial.number` filter to force the `getLowerFilters()` path that includes all primary filters but no secondary filters, causing the multi-union query to return all NFT transaction records for all serial numbers of tokens above X?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp` filter array with the maximum allowed repeated parameters to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()`, causing `updateQueryFiltersWithInValues()` to build a massive `IN()` clause that retrieves staking reward transactions at many specific timestamps simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id` with `lte` operator and no `serial.number` filter to force `getUpperFilters()` to return an empty array (since `secondary.hasUpper()` is false), causing the multi-union query to silently ignore the upper bound and return more NFT transaction records than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id=gt:0` filter to `spenderIdFilters` to cause `NftService.getSubQuery()` to append a condition `spender > $N` to every sub-query in the union, effectively filtering out NFT transaction records where no spender is set and hiding legitimate unapproved NFT transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=eq:X` filter multiple times (as an array) to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()`, causing `timestampInValues` to accumulate multiple equal values that get passed to `updateQueryFiltersWithInValues()`, and observe whether the response reveals staking reward transactions at those exact consensus timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id=gte:X` and `serial.number=gte:Y` to force the `lower` sub-query to use `{...primary.lower, operator: opsMap.eq}` (converting `gte` to `eq`), causing the query to return NFT transaction records only for the exact token ID X rather than all tokens >= X, potentially hiding transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id=lte:X` and `serial.number=lte:Y` to force `getUpperFilters()` to return `[{...primary.upper, operator: opsMap.eq}, secondary.upper]`, causing the upper sub-query to use `token.id = X` instead of `token.id <= X`, potentially returning fewer NFT transaction records than expected and masking transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=gt:X` filter to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()` where X is a known consensus timestamp of a specific transaction, to enumerate all staking reward transactions that occurred after that transaction, enabling precise transaction ordering analysis?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted EVM address (40 hex chars) as `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` that matches a known contract's EVM address to retrieve NFT transaction records associated with that contract, bypassing the intended account-only scope of the endpoint?
